Canada

University of Toronto Scholarships for Migrant and Refugee Students in Canada

Published 11 Jun 20265 min read✍ MigrantScholar Team✓ Verified 11 Jun 2026
Advertisement

Introduction to University of Toronto Scholarships for Migrant and Refugee Students in Canada

The University of Toronto offers various scholarships to migrant and refugee students in Canada, providing financial assistance to help them pursue their academic goals. These scholarships are designed to support students who have been displaced due to conflict, persecution, or natural disasters, and are now rebuilding their lives in Canada. If you are a migrant or refugee student looking to study at the University of Toronto, you may be eligible for one of these scholarships.

Who Qualifies

To be eligible for University of Toronto scholarships, migrant and refugee students must hold one of the following visa categories:

* Convention Refugee

* Protected Person

* Refugee Claimant

* Permanent Resident with refugee background

* Temporary Resident Permit holder with refugee background

* Other visa categories may also be eligible, but these are the most common ones.

Available Scholarships

The University of Toronto offers several scholarships to migrant and refugee students, including:

University of Toronto Scholars Program

The University of Toronto Scholars Program provides a scholarship of up to $10,000 per year, renewable for four years. To be eligible, students must be entering their first year of undergraduate studies, have a minimum admission average of 95%, and demonstrate financial need. The deadline to apply is February 1st. For more information, visit https://www.utsc.utoronto.ca/current-students/financial-aid.

President's Scholars of Excellence Program

The President's Scholars of Excellence Program provides a scholarship of up to $10,000 per year, renewable for four years. To be eligible, students must be entering their first year of undergraduate studies, have a minimum admission average of 95%, and demonstrate academic excellence. The deadline to apply is February 1st. For more information, visit https://www.artsci.utoronto.ca/current-students/financial-aid.

University of Toronto Fellowship

The University of Toronto Fellowship provides a scholarship of up to $20,000 per year, renewable for two years. To be eligible, students must be entering their first year of graduate studies, have a minimum admission average of A-, and demonstrate academic excellence. The deadline to apply is January 15th. For more information, visit https://www.sgs.utoronto.ca/funding-awards.

Mastercard Foundation Scholars Program

The Mastercard Foundation Scholars Program provides a scholarship of up to $50,000 per year, renewable for four years. To be eligible, students must be entering their first year of undergraduate studies, have a minimum admission average of 80%, and demonstrate financial need. The deadline to apply is February 1st. For more information, visit https://www(mastercardfdn.org/scholars.

Ontario Graduate Scholarship

The Ontario Graduate Scholarship provides a scholarship of up to $15,000 per year, renewable for two years. To be eligible, students must be entering their first year of graduate studies, have a minimum admission average of A-, and demonstrate academic excellence. The deadline to apply is January 15th. For more information, visit https://www.sgs.utoronto.ca/funding-awards.

Application Steps

To apply for University of Toronto scholarships, follow these steps:

  • Check your eligibility for the scholarship you are interested in.

  • Review the application requirements and deadlines.

  • Submit your application through the University of Toronto's online application portal.

  • Upload all required documents, including transcripts, letters of reference, and proof of language proficiency.

  • Pay the application fee, if required.

  • Submit your application by the deadline.

  • Wait for the scholarship committee to review your application.

  • Receive notification of the outcome of your application.

Documents Checklist

To apply for University of Toronto scholarships, you will need to provide the following documents:

* Transcripts from all previous academic institutions

* Letters of reference from academic or professional referees

* Proof of language proficiency (e.g. IELTS, TOEFL)

* Proof of financial need (e.g. tax returns, bank statements)

* Proof of refugee status (e.g. refugee claimant document, Convention Refugee document)

* Other documents may be required, depending on the scholarship you are applying for.

Frequently Asked Questions

What is the deadline to apply for University of Toronto scholarships?

The deadline to apply for University of Toronto scholarships varies depending on the scholarship, but most deadlines are in February or January. Check the official website of the University of Toronto for the most up-to-date information.

Can I apply for multiple scholarships at the same time?

Yes, you can apply for multiple scholarships at the same time, but you must meet the eligibility requirements for each scholarship.

Do I need to have a high GPA to be eligible for University of Toronto scholarships?

While a high GPA is not always required, it is often a competitive factor in the scholarship selection process. Check the eligibility requirements for each scholarship to see what GPA is required.

Can I apply for a University of Toronto scholarship if I am not a Canadian citizen?

Yes, many University of Toronto scholarships are open to international students, including migrant and refugee students. Check the eligibility requirements for each scholarship to see if you are eligible.

How do I apply for a University of Toronto scholarship?

To apply for a University of Toronto scholarship, you must submit an application through the University of Toronto's online application portal.

What documents do I need to provide to apply for a University of Toronto scholarship?

You will need to provide transcripts, letters of reference, proof of language proficiency, and other documents, depending on the scholarship you are applying for.

Can I appeal a decision if my scholarship application is denied?

Yes, you can appeal a decision if your scholarship application is denied, but you must follow the appeal process outlined by the University of Toronto.

How long does it take to receive a decision on my scholarship application?

The time it takes to receive a decision on your scholarship application varies, but it is usually within a few weeks or months after the deadline.

For more information on University of Toronto scholarships, visit the official website of the University of Toronto at https://www.utoronto.ca or the official government website at https://www.canada.ca.

To learn more about scholarships and other resources available to migrant and refugee students, visit https://migrantscholar.vercel.app/blog.
